raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a formal complaint against Barclays Bank regarding the improper closure of my dispute appeal due to Barclays failure to properly process and log documents that were submitted on time. 
Barclays issued a letter dated XX/XX/year>, stating that I had 10 days to submit documentation to appeal a dispute decision. However, I did not receive this letter until XX/XX/year>, which significantly reduced the response window. 
To ensure compliance, I contacted Barclays on XX/XX/year>, to confirm whether I could still submit my appeal documents. I was advised by a Barclays representative that I could proceed with submitting the documentation. I faxed all requested appeal documents on XX/XX/year>, the same day. 
Despite this, Barclays closed my dispute without notice. When I contacted Barclays on XX/XX/year>, I was informed that the back office did not receive my faxed documents. However, the representative confirmed that my XX/XX/year> phone call was documented in Barclays system, demonstrating that I acted in good faith and within the permitted timeframe. 
Barclays internal failure to log or process submitted documents should not result in denial of a consumers right to appeal. I am requesting that the dispute be reopened immediately, my appeal reviewed without prejudice, and written confirmation provided regarding the current status of my case. 
This complaint is submitted due to concerns regarding improper dispute handling, lack of reasonable follow-up, and failure to protect consumer rights.
